name: Herman's Famous Kosher Dills
recipeIngredient: 3 level tablespoons + 1 level teaspoon pickling spices (remove red peppers)
1 clove thinly sliced garlic (or more to taste)
¼ teaspoon powdered or granulated alum
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the pickles and pack them halfway into the jar.
2. Add all remaining ingredients to the jar.
3. Finish packing with cold tap water, sealing the jar tightly.
4. Invert the jar several times to dissolve the ingredients.
5. Let stand at room temperature for 24 hours before refrigerating.
6. The pickles are ready to eat in two days (for half sour); let them stand longer for increased sourness.
7. Salt and vinegar can be adjusted to personal preference.
8. To make pickled green tomatoes, use the same recipe but add a 2–3 inch piece of fresh celery stalk; let stand at room temperature for 5 weeks before refrigerating.
9. Do not use a bare metal lid; it will rust from the vinegar and salt, giving an off taste.
